title: echium tuberculatum subsp. latifolium
intro: Echium tuberculatum subsp. latifolium, also known as broad-leaved purple viper's bugloss, is a plant species that belongs to the borage family (Boraginaceae). Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is rich in flavonoids and phenolic compounds, providing various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and exhibiting antioxidant properties. In herbal preparations, Echium tuberculatum subsp. latifolium is often used to make infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to treat various ailments, including wounds and skin conditions.
